titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Winifred Welles Papers
titlestmt.author: by Janet Lopes

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Monday March 24, 2025 at 23:19

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Gift of Gail-Marie Shearer Trust (trustee Peter Shearer), 2024.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged into three series: I. Writings, 1906-1942. II. Correspondence, 1917-1975. III. Personal and professional papers, 1688-1985.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Winifred Welles (1893-1939) was an American writer known for her poetry and prose. Born in Norwich, Connecticut, she authored several books including The Hesitant Heart , This Delicate Love , and The Lost Landscape: Some Memories of a Family and a Town in Connecticut, 1659-1906 . Welles' poetry was also published in several magazines such as Harper's Magazine and The New Yorker . She was a frequent correspondent of other 20th century writers such as H. L. Mencken, Louise Townsend Nicholl, Lawrence Gilman, Christopher Morley and Laura Benét.
Welles was working on a novel about Emily Dickinson before she died. Come Slowly, Eden was completed by author Laura Benét.
Welles' was married to Harold Hutchison Shearer and had one child, James Welles Shearer.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Winifred Welles papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Winifred Welles papers contain materials related to the author's work in poetry and fiction. It includes early drafts, manuscripts, transcripts, and correspondence with early 20th century writers including H. L. Mencken, Louise Townsend Nicholl, Lawrence Gilman, and Christopher Morley. Included in the papers is a draft of an unfinished manuscript about Emily Dickinson, completed after Welles' death by the author Laura Benét.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Winifred Welles papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Writings are arranged alphabetically by title when possible, with uncollected writings placed at the end of the series.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains drafts, manuscripts, and typescripts of poetry and prose by Winfred Welles. It includes the manuscript for an unfinished book on Emily Dickinson, which was finished by author Laura Benét after Welles' death.

did.unittitle: "The Little Boy Who Was Sent Away by his Mother", typescript
"The Lost Landscape", annotated typescripts and notes

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Written by W. W. Welles at the age of 13.

did.unittitle: "From Miss Miranda's Four Post Bed", typescripts
Notebook of manuscript poems and laid-in pages
Manuscript and correspondence for book finished by author Laura Benet
Emily Dickinson notebook, notes, and writings
Notes and beginning of mystery story, manuscript
Journal of poems and laid in material
Miscellaneous poems, partial and full annotated manuscripts and typescripts
Children's verse and various writings, manuscripts and typescripts
Miscellaneous literary notes, small notebooks, typescripts, book titles, and clippings
Manuscripts, typescripts, notes with laid-in papers

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters to and about Winifred Welles from her husband, Harold Hutchinson Shearer, various author friends, and publishers.

did.unittitle: Letters to Winifred Welles from husband, Harold Hutchinson Shearer
Letters regarding Winifred Welles' work from various editors and publishers
Letter to Russell Welles, Winifred Welles' brother
Letters about tracking down historical data for "L. L."
Paths of Glory , book and laid in correspondence from author Humphrey Cobb and others
Letter to Philip A. Johnson from James Welles Shearer
Copy of will of James C. Russell establishing trust for Winifred Welles and her issue
Typescript letters and "God's Goodness Towards Martha Coit"
Address books with laid in materials
Dr. Philip Turner of Norwich, Connecticut, Annals of Medical History

title.part: Block Island: A Hand-Book with Map

did.unittitle: Bound notebook with laid in material
Emily Dickinson, Face to Face , page
